{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior Finance Manager",
  "description": "J3 is an FMCG distributor. We have been in existence over 8 years.&nbsp;J3 has branches in several locations; Lagos, Ibadan, Ogun, Ondo, and Osun state.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Finance Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Iyana Ipaja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Ensure all financial transactions and records are accurately maintained for J3 Foods and Stores International.
	Ensure timely and accurate preparation of financial statements (monthly, quarterly, annual) for all outlets.
	Ensure accurate reconciliation of inventory transactions (purchases, movements, write-offs) in alignment with accounting standards.
	Collaborate with the warehouse department to validate stock reports and minimize discrepancies between physical and book inventory.
	Review and approve balance sheet reconciliations (bank, inventory, receivables, payables).
	Manage and mentor Account Officers across all distribution outlets.
	Conduct monthly performance reviews and ensure adherence to accounting procedures.
	mplement standardized accounting processes across all locations.
	Resolve complex accounting issues escalated by Account Officers.
	Approve payment vouchers, petty cash, and bank reconciliations.
	Ensure payroll is processed correctly and on time, including accurate deductions and remittances.
	Manage and monitor payment systems to verify incoming payments.
	Oversee cost accounting and ensure proper allocation of expenses.
	Implement cost-saving measures without compromising operational efficiency.
	Monitor trade promotions, discounts, and rebate accounting.
	Validate sales reports and ensure revenue recognition aligns with accounting standards.
	Ensure timely filing of VAT, corporate taxes, and other statutory obligations.
	Stay updated on changes in tax laws affecting FMCG distribution.
	Implement internal controls to prevent fraud and errors.
	Train Account Officers on new accounting processes and policies.
	Prepare financial reports to MD/CEO and General manager.
	Advise the management on investment opportunities and ways to improve profitability.


Salary
N400,000 / Month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;midaasconsult@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the email.

Note:&nbsp;For more enquiries, send a WhatsApp message to:09152781100.",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-04",
  "validThrough": "2026-04-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "J3 Foods and Store International"
    
  },

    "@type": "Place",

      "@type": "PostalAddress",

       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Lagos",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",
    "value": "195381"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 400000,
      "maxValue": 600000,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }
  }
}



Senior Finance Manager Job at J3 Foods and Store International




        
    

        
        J3 is an FMCG distributor. We have been in existence over 8 years. J3 has branches in several locations; Lagos, Ibadan, Ogun, Ondo, and Osun state.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Finance Manager

Job Location: Iyana Ipaja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Ensure all financial transactions and records are accurately maintained for J3 Foods and Stores International.
	Ensure timely and accurate preparation of financial statements (monthly, quarterly, annual) for all outlets.
	Ensure accurate reconciliation of inventory transactions (purchases, movements, write-offs) in alignment with accounting standards.
	Collaborate with the warehouse department to validate stock reports and minimize discrepancies between physical and book inventory.
	Review and approve balance sheet reconciliations (bank, inventory, receivables, payables).
	Manage and mentor Account Officers across all distribution outlets.
	Conduct monthly performance reviews and ensure adherence to accounting procedures.
	mplement standardized accounting processes across all locations.
	Resolve complex accounting issues escalated by Account Officers.
	Approve payment vouchers, petty cash, and bank reconciliations.
	Ensure payroll is processed correctly and on time, including accurate deductions and remittances.
	Manage and monitor payment systems to verify incoming payments.
	Oversee cost accounting and ensure proper allocation of expenses.
	Implement cost-saving measures without compromising operational efficiency.
	Monitor trade promotions, discounts, and rebate accounting.
	Validate sales reports and ensure revenue recognition aligns with accounting standards.
	Ensure timely filing of VAT, corporate taxes, and other statutory obligations.
	Stay updated on changes in tax laws affecting FMCG distribution.
	Implement internal controls to prevent fraud and errors.
	Train Account Officers on new accounting processes and policies.
	Prepare financial reports to MD/CEO and General manager.
	Advise the management on investment opportunities and ways to improve profitability.


Salary
N400,000 / Month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: midaasconsult@gmail.com using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the email.

Note: For more enquiries, send a WhatsApp message to:09152781100.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Inventory Reconciliation Specialist",
  "description": "Agu.ng is a nationally recognized fashion retailer owned and managed by Agurate Online Limited. Agurate Online Limited was incorporated under the Nigerian laws by the Corporate affairs commission in August 2015. We&rsquo;re passionate about Fashion retail both online and offline &ndash; and it goes way beyond fashion.

At Agu.ng, fashion is a way of life. We provide the best fashion materials and accesories. We are located in Onitsha Mall, Enugu mall, Novare mall lekki, Novare Gateway Mall Abuja and Circle mall Osapa. As we strive to bring your lovely fashion to your neighborhood, you can always shop on our website.
We differentiate ourselves through a combination of unique designs and styles merged with unequaled standards of quality and authenticity. Our founders Agurate Online limited is a company built on hardwork, fairness and customer satisfaction.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Reconciliation Specialist

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type:&nbsp;Full-time

Job summary


	The Inventory Reconciliation Specialist is responsible for ensuring the accuracy and integrity of inventory records across all stores and the e-commerce platform.
	The role focuses on stock reconciliation, variance analysis, shrinkage control, and strengthening inventory governance processes.
	The ideal candidate must possess strong analytical skills, deep understanding of inventory controls, and exceptional reconciliation capability.


Key Responsibilities
Inventory Reconciliation &amp; Counting:


	Perform daily, weekly, and monthly reconciliation of physical stock against ERP/system records.
	Reconcile warehouse, store, and e-commerce inventory balances.
	Partake in monthly and periodic stock count.
	Investigate and resolve stock discrepancies, shrinkage, and transfer variances.
	Validate Goods Received Notes (GRNs), stock transfers, returns, and adjustments.
	Ensure alignment between finance records and inventory movement reports.


Variance Analysis &amp; Reporting:


	Prepare inventory variance reports and provide root cause analysis.
	Monitor stock movement trends and identify unusual patterns.
	Track shrinkage levels and recommend corrective actions.
	Provide monthly inventory accuracy reports to management.


Process Control &amp; Compliance:


	Enforce adherence to inventory management policies and SOPs.
	Conduct periodic spot checks and support physical stock counts.
	Strengthen internal controls to reduce stock loss and fraud exposure.
	Collaborate with Store Supervisors and Warehouse Officers to improve stock accuracy.


Systems &amp; Data Management:


	Ensure accurate data entry for stock movements within ERP systems.
	Support automation initiatives to improve inventory visibility.
	Maintain reconciliation schedules and audit-ready documentation


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Supply Chain, Business Administration, or related field.
	3&ndash;5 years of experience in inventory control, warehouse accounting, or stock audit.
	Retail experience will be an added advantage.
	Experience using ERP or inventory management systems.
	Knowledge of stock audit procedures and internal control frameworks.


Salary
N250,000 / month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@agu.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-01",

  },

Inventory Reconciliation Specialist Job at Agurate Online Limited




        
    

        
        Agu.ng is a nationally recognized fashion retailer owned and managed by Agurate Online Limited. Agurate Online Limited was incorporated under the Nigerian laws by the Corporate affairs commission in August 2015. We’re passionate about Fashion retail both online and offline – and it goes way beyond fashion.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



At Agu.ng, fashion is a way of life. We provide the best fashion materials and accesories. We are located in Onitsha Mall, Enugu mall, Novare mall lekki, Novare Gateway Mall Abuja and Circle mall Osapa. As we strive to bring your lovely fashion to your neighborhood, you can always shop on our website.
We differentiate ourselves through a combination of unique designs and styles merged with unequaled standards of quality and authenticity. Our founders Agurate Online limited is a company built on hardwork, fairness and customer satisfaction.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Reconciliation Specialist

Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job summary


	The Inventory Reconciliation Specialist is responsible for ensuring the accuracy and integrity of inventory records across all stores and the e-commerce platform.
	The role focuses on stock reconciliation, variance analysis, shrinkage control, and strengthening inventory governance processes.
	The ideal candidate must possess strong analytical skills, deep understanding of inventory controls, and exceptional reconciliation capability.


Key Responsibilities
Inventory Reconciliation & Counting:


	Perform daily, weekly, and monthly reconciliation of physical stock against ERP/system records.
	Reconcile warehouse, store, and e-commerce inventory balances.
	Partake in monthly and periodic stock count.
	Investigate and resolve stock discrepancies, shrinkage, and transfer variances.
	Validate Goods Received Notes (GRNs), stock transfers, returns, and adjustments.
	Ensure alignment between finance records and inventory movement reports.


Variance Analysis & Reporting:


	Prepare inventory variance reports and provide root cause analysis.
	Monitor stock movement trends and identify unusual patterns.
	Track shrinkage levels and recommend corrective actions.
	Provide monthly inventory accuracy reports to management.


Process Control & Compliance:


	Enforce adherence to inventory management policies and SOPs.
	Conduct periodic spot checks and support physical stock counts.
	Strengthen internal controls to reduce stock loss and fraud exposure.
	Collaborate with Store Supervisors and Warehouse Officers to improve stock accuracy.


Systems & Data Management:


	Ensure accurate data entry for stock movements within ERP systems.
	Support automation initiatives to improve inventory visibility.
	Maintain reconciliation schedules and audit-ready documentation


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Supply Chain, Business Administration, or related field.
	3–5 years of experience in inventory control, warehouse accounting, or stock audit.
	Retail experience will be an added advantage.
	Experience using ERP or inventory management systems.
	Knowledge of stock audit procedures and internal control frameworks.


Salary
N250,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@agu.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the mail    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Property Manager",
  "description": "Voedsel Capital Limited is a team of young, dynamic and goal-driven individuals, who have a common goal to improve the lives of our clients through access to Finance. Voedsel Capital Limited is focused on improving the productivity of Micro, Small and Medium-scale businesses that have displayed evidence of traction and possess huge growth potential but do not have access to traditional financing solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Property Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary


	We are seeking a qualified and experienced Property Manager to join our real estate and property management team.


&nbsp;Key Responsibilities


	Day-to-day management of residential and commercial properties.
	Coordination of maintenance, repairs, and service providers.
	Rent collection, service charge administration, and reporting to property owners.
	Tenant management, inspections, and compliance with lease terms.
	Preparation of property management reports and documentation.


Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	BSc or HND qualificatiojn in Estate Management and Valuation from a recognized institution
	Advanced training and hands-on experience in managing multi-residential and commercial properties.
	Strong knowledge of tenancy administration, rent collection, service charge management, and property maintenance.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and reporting skills.
	Member of the Nigerian Institution of Estate Surveyors and Valuers (NIESV) is an added advantage
	Ability to work independently and manage multiple properties efficiently.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@voedselng.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as&nbsp;the subject of the mail.",

Property Manager Job at Voedsel Capital Limited




        
    

        
        Voedsel Capital Limited is a team of young, dynamic and goal-driven individuals, who have a common goal to improve the lives of our clients through access to Finance. Voedsel Capital Limited is focused on improving the productivity of Micro, Small and Medium-scale businesses that have displayed evidence of traction and possess huge growth potential but do not have access to traditional financing solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Property Manager

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a qualified and experienced Property Manager to join our real estate and property management team.


 Key Responsibilities


	Day-to-day management of residential and commercial properties.
	Coordination of maintenance, repairs, and service providers.
	Rent collection, service charge administration, and reporting to property owners.
	Tenant management, inspections, and compliance with lease terms.
	Preparation of property management reports and documentation.


Qualifications & Requirements


	BSc or HND qualificatiojn in Estate Management and Valuation from a recognized institution
	Advanced training and hands-on experience in managing multi-residential and commercial properties.
	Strong knowledge of tenancy administration, rent collection, service charge management, and property maintenance.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and reporting skills.
	Member of the Nigerian Institution of Estate Surveyors and Valuers (NIESV) is an added advantage
	Ability to work independently and manage multiple properties efficiently.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@voedselng.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operations Manager",
  "description": "J3 Foods and Store International&nbsp;is an FMCG distributor. We have been in existence over 8 years.&nbsp;J3 has branches in several locations; Lagos, Ibadan, Ogun, Ondo, and Osun state.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Manager
Job Location:&nbsp;Iyana Ipaja - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	The Operations Manager oversees the daily operations of assigned outlets in Lagos, ensuring smooth distribution, inventory accuracy, team performance, and customer satisfaction, all in alignment with the J3 foods and stores international goals.
	This role serves as the direct representative of senior leadership across these branches, particularly in the absence of dedicated Branch Managers.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise, train, and manage all staff across the assigned outlets (warehouse, sales, cashier, delivery), ensuring efficient operations across all departments.
	Monitor daily activities across all outlets to ensure compliance with company standards, operational procedures, and policy guidelines.
	Conduct regular performance reviews and provide ongoing feedback, coaching, and disciplinary guidance where necessary.
	Monitor and support the marketing team in meeting targets for direct buyers, bulk buyers &amp; walk in customers. 5. Collaborate with the marketing team to develop and implement weekly and monthly marketing and sales plans per outlet, and track progress.
	Track progress on sales targets across outlets and provide strategic guidance or adjustments to ensure goals are met.
	Ensure accurate daily reconciliation of sales and payments across all outlets under your supervision.
	Review and validate all stock movement (inward and outward), ensuring all inventory activities are properly documented.
	Supervise the delivery and return processes to minimize product damage, loss, and stock discrepancies.
	Conduct outlet visits, review outlet conditions (cleanliness, organization, safety), and escalate facility needs to HR/Admin where necessary. 1
	Act as the liaison between outlet teams and head office leadership, ensuring smooth communication, prompt escalation of issues, and consistent feedback.


KPIs


	Sales Volume Growth: Monthly and quarterly sales target achievement across assigned outlets.
	Stock Accuracy: Low variance between system records and physical stock.
	Sales Reconciliation Compliance: 100% daily sales and payment reconciliation across all branches.
	Customer satisfaction (complaints resolved within 24hrs).
	Operational Discipline: Outlet cleanliness, staff punctuality, and SOP compliance.
	Number of active customers retained and reactivated.


Results Expected:


	Consistent and smooth operations across all Lagos outlets under supervision.
	Growth in sales volume and achievement of monthly and quarterly sales targets.
	Accurate and timely reconciliation of daily sales and payment records.
	Minimal stock variances through effective inventory monitoring and documentation.
	Improved customer satisfaction, with complaints resolved promptly (within 24 hours).
	Retention and reactivation of customers, contributing to increased customer loyalty and revenue.
	Operational discipline maintained across outlets, including staff punctuality, cleanliness, and SOP adherence.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications with experience.


Salary
N150,000 - N160,000 / month

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;midaasconsult@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the mail.

Note:&nbsp;For more enquiries, send a WhatsApp message to: 09152781100.",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

  },

Operations Manager Job at J3 Foods and Store International




        
    

        
        J3 Foods and Store International is an FMCG distributor. We have been in existence over 8 years. J3 has branches in several locations; Lagos, Ibadan, Ogun, Ondo, and Osun state.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Manager
Job Location: Iyana Ipaja - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Operations Manager oversees the daily operations of assigned outlets in Lagos, ensuring smooth distribution, inventory accuracy, team performance, and customer satisfaction, all in alignment with the J3 foods and stores international goals.
	This role serves as the direct representative of senior leadership across these branches, particularly in the absence of dedicated Branch Managers.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise, train, and manage all staff across the assigned outlets (warehouse, sales, cashier, delivery), ensuring efficient operations across all departments.
	Monitor daily activities across all outlets to ensure compliance with company standards, operational procedures, and policy guidelines.
	Conduct regular performance reviews and provide ongoing feedback, coaching, and disciplinary guidance where necessary.
	Monitor and support the marketing team in meeting targets for direct buyers, bulk buyers & walk in customers. 5. Collaborate with the marketing team to develop and implement weekly and monthly marketing and sales plans per outlet, and track progress.
	Track progress on sales targets across outlets and provide strategic guidance or adjustments to ensure goals are met.
	Ensure accurate daily reconciliation of sales and payments across all outlets under your supervision.
	Review and validate all stock movement (inward and outward), ensuring all inventory activities are properly documented.
	Supervise the delivery and return processes to minimize product damage, loss, and stock discrepancies.
	Conduct outlet visits, review outlet conditions (cleanliness, organization, safety), and escalate facility needs to HR/Admin where necessary. 1
	Act as the liaison between outlet teams and head office leadership, ensuring smooth communication, prompt escalation of issues, and consistent feedback.


KPIs


	Sales Volume Growth: Monthly and quarterly sales target achievement across assigned outlets.
	Stock Accuracy: Low variance between system records and physical stock.
	Sales Reconciliation Compliance: 100% daily sales and payment reconciliation across all branches.
	Customer satisfaction (complaints resolved within 24hrs).
	Operational Discipline: Outlet cleanliness, staff punctuality, and SOP compliance.
	Number of active customers retained and reactivated.


Results Expected:


	Consistent and smooth operations across all Lagos outlets under supervision.
	Growth in sales volume and achievement of monthly and quarterly sales targets.
	Accurate and timely reconciliation of daily sales and payment records.
	Minimal stock variances through effective inventory monitoring and documentation.
	Improved customer satisfaction, with complaints resolved promptly (within 24 hours).
	Retention and reactivation of customers, contributing to increased customer loyalty and revenue.
	Operational discipline maintained across outlets, including staff punctuality, cleanliness, and SOP adherence.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications with experience.


Salary
N150,000 - N160,000 / month

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: midaasconsult@gmail.com using the Job Position and Location as the subject of the mail.

Note: For more enquiries, send a WhatsApp message to: 09152781100.
